sourceScopeConflicts.tierAssignmentRationale: Page is on health-system domain my.clevelandclinic.org and content explicitly addresses Ohio main campus, not the named Florida institution.
sourceScopeConflicts.limitations: Institution under investigation is Cleveland Clinic Florida; this fellowship is hosted at Cleveland Clinic Ohio main campus.

unresolveds: Page is on health-system domain (my.clevelandclinic.org) and describes the Cleveland, Ohio Pain Medicine Fellowship; not clearly applicable to Cleveland Clinic Florida campus.
recommendedA2Focus: Confirm whether Cleveland Clinic Florida has its own Pain Medicine Fellowship or whether Florida-based applicants rotate through the Ohio program.
Locate Cleveland Clinic Florida-specific GME/fellowship listings to validate campus applicability.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The Pediatric Anesthesiology Fellowship Program at Cleveland Clinic was established in 1999 under the leadership of Dr. Julie Niezgoda, the section head for pediatric anesthesiology, and Dr. Emad Mossad, the section head for congenital heart anesthesiology.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Cleveland Clinic offers a Pediatric Anesthesiology Fellowship Program established in 1999.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Page describes a subspecialty fellowship program (post-residency training).
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Page is on my.clevelandclinic.org and describes the Cleveland, Ohio main-campus fellowship; institution is Cleveland Clinic Florida — scope conflict.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The Pediatric Anesthesiology fellowship offers three positions each year. All positions are filled through the San Franscico Match (ACGME Program ID PR10110). Applications are accepted from January until May 31st.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Three fellow positions per year; filled via SF Match (ACGME ID PR10110); applications accepted Jan-May 31.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Application details for a subspecialty fellowship.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Applications should be submitted via SF match and will include the following materials:CVPersonal statementUSMLE and/or COMLEX scoresThree letters of recommendation (one must be from the Program Director)ECFMG Certificate (if applicable)ABA in-training scores and Basic Exam score
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Required application materials include CV, personal statement, USMLE/COMLEX, 3 LORs, ECFMG (if applicable), ABA scores.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship application requirements including ECFMG indicates IMG-eligible signal at trainee tier.

futureLaneSignals.quote: We offer flexible start dates, with the majority of our fellows starting either July 1st or August 1st.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Fellowship offers flexible start dates, typically July 1 or August 1.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship start-date logistics.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Lauren Licina, MDDirector, Pediatric Anesthesiology Fellowship ProgramPhone: 216.445-7455 | Fax: 216.444.9247Email: Licinal@ccf.orgKassie Fedor, BBAProgram CoordinatorEmail: Fedork2@ccf.org
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Program Director Lauren Licina (Licinal@ccf.org, 216.445-7455); Coordinator Kassie Fedor (Fedork2@ccf.org).
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship program contact info.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Fellows have the opportunity to assist in teaching medical students and anesthesiology residents during dedicated pediatric simulation sessions and in clinical cases.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Fellows teach medical students and anesthesiology residents during pediatric simulations and clinical cases.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Describes fellow teaching role; mention of medical students is incidental, not a USCE offering.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Mention of medical students refers to fellows teaching them, not an offer for visiting students.

recommendedA2Focus: Verify whether this Pediatric Anesthesia Fellowship is hosted at Cleveland Clinic Florida or Cleveland Clinic main campus (Ohio) — page footer and 216 area codes indicate Ohio.
